L1.area: File system operations, I/O handling, and resource cleanup utilities in the core module

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in a recursive file deletion routine that does not validate whether resolved file paths remain within an intended base directory. An attacker can supply specially crafted inputs containing directory traversal sequences to escape the target directory and cause deletion of arbitrary files on the system.

L3.area: Cache cleaning and file deletion utilities in the core application module
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in a recursive file deletion method used during cache cleaning operations that does not verify whether resolved file paths remain within the allowed cache base directory. An attacker can craft directory traversal sequences that escape the intended cache directory, leading to unauthorized deletion of arbitrary files on the system.

ground_truth.reason: The recursive file deletion method accepts file paths without verifying they remain within the intended base directory, allowing directory traversal sequences to delete arbitrary files on the system.
